subject: Computer Networking
title: Service Workers
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Service Workers.

hook.text: What if your web app worked offline? Service workers intercept network requests and serve cached responses.

buildup.text: A service worker is JavaScript that runs in the background, separate from the page. It acts as a proxy.

discovery.text: On install, it pre-caches critical files. On fetch, it serves from cache first, then updates from the network.

twist.text: Service workers power push notifications and background sync too. They make web apps feel native.

climax.text: PWAs — Progressive Web Apps — use service workers to install on your home screen and work offline.

punchline.text: Service workers make the web work offline. Apps without the app store.

quiz.question: What role does a service worker play in a web app?
options: It intercepts network requests and can serve cached responses
It compiles JavaScript into native code
It manages the browser's tab layout
